summary: ForgeSlicer iter 54 — ARM64 OrcaSlicer support FULLY VERIFIED. All 4 targeted backend endpoints pass on the live preview pod; the E2E cube slice produces real gcode (125 KB / 5027 lines / 50 layers); the frontend Slicer popover now offers OrcaSlicer as a selectable engine (no longer 'unsupported on aarch64'); and the existing pytest regression suite (37 orca-adjacent tests across iter15/staging/engine/install/fallback) is green.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: orca_engine.py has grown to 1000+ lines — fine for now but consider splitting profile-staging, install, and slice-runner into sibling modules in a future iter.
Frontend SlicerPopover.jsx — orcaReady flag relies entirely on /api/slice/orca/status; consider adding a 'last polled' timestamp so users can manually retry status without reloading.
test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md mongo-seed pattern. Created ephemeral session st_test_1780068655060 for user_test1780068655060 — DELETED at end of run.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test user + session (deleted post-test). No persistent test data.

evidence.POST /api/slice/orca/slice (cube 10mm): 200 — gcode 125481 bytes / 5027 lines / 50 layers / 245.34mm filament / 0.12s, contains G1, M104/M109, OrcaSlicer 2.3.2 header
evidence.POST /api/slice/orca/reinstall: 200 — {status:'started', argv:['bash','/app/backend/scripts/install_orca_arm64.sh'], lock_file:'/app/backend/bin/.orca_install_lock'} — confirms aarch64 routing to flatpak installer (previously rejected with 400 'AppImage is x86_64-only')
evidence.GET /api/slice/orca/preset?vendor=OrcaFi: 200 — preset.from='system' confirmed (validates base/ subdir lookup in _load_system_preset for v2.3.x layout)
evidence.Frontend Slicer popover: menu-slicer-btn opens popover; slicer-engine-orca button is enabled, label 'OrcaSlicer / server-side', clickable, becomes active (bg-purple-500/15 border-purple-500/60). No 'unsupported on aarch64' string in DOM.
evidence.pytest regression: 37/37 PASS — test_iter15_smoke.py, test_orca_profile_staging.py, test_orca_engine.py, test_install_orca.py, test_orca_fallback_preset.py

context_for_next_testing_agent: ARM64 OrcaSlicer is live on the preview pod via flatpak runtime. Binary at /app/backend/bin/orca-aarch64/OrcaSlicer is a bash launcher that exec's ld-linux-aarch64.so.1 against the flatpak share. /api/slice/orca/status returns arch='aarch64', source='app-aarch64', version='OrcaSlicer-2.3.2:'. Frontend SlicerPopover uses orcaReady = orcaStatus?.installed === true to gate the slicer-engine-orca button — currently true on the preview. The smoke test /app/backend/tests/test_orca_arm64_slice.py exercises the full slice path (cube STL → gcode) and is the canonical regression for this engine. To repeat the workspace UI test, seed a mongo session per /app/memory/test_credentials.md and inject session_token cookie before navigating to /workspace.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. iter 54 lands cleanly. Note: status.version string ends with a trailing colon ('OrcaSlicer-2.3.2:') because the upstream launcher echoes 'OrcaSlicer-2.3.2: <buildhash>' and the parser keeps the prefix-with-colon; cosmetic only, smoke test asserts startswith('OrcaSlicer-2.3.') which is the documented contract.
